sectionSubtitle: Six exchange markets launching at HeadyEx — each representing a fundamental resource category in the AI compute economy, with standardized units, real-time pricing, and transparent volume data.
disclaimer: HeadyEx is a computational resource credit exchange, not a financial or securities exchange. All prices are for AI compute and data resources.

markets.name: AI API Credits
markets.description: Heady API credits are the primary currency of the HeadyEx exchange. Each credit represents a standardized quantum of Heady API compute — equivalent to 1,000 input tokens + 500 output tokens at base model quality. Organizations with surplus monthly allocations list excess credits; teams with spiky or unexpected demand source additional capacity at market rates. Credit lots are available in denominations of 1K, 10K, 100K, and 1M credits.

markets.name: Embedding Compute
markets.description: Embedding compute units represent the cost to generate vector embeddings for text, images, or multimodal content. HeadyEx standardizes embedding compute across multiple model providers (OpenAI text-embedding-3, Voyage AI, Cohere, and Heady's internal embedding model) into a common Embedding Compute Unit (ECU) — 1 ECU = embedding 1M tokens at 1536 dimensions. Trade ECU lots to balance embedding workloads across providers.

markets.name: Vector Storage
markets.description: Vector Storage Units (VSUs) represent capacity in the HeadyCloud pgvector infrastructure. 1 VSU = 1 GB of vector storage at 1536-dimensional embeddings (approximately 130,000 vectors). Organizations can sell excess provisioned storage and buy additional capacity without changing their HeadyCloud plan tier. Storage is served from the purchaser's preferred HeadyCloud region.

markets.name: GPU Burst
markets.description: GPU Burst Units (GBUs) represent spot GPU capacity on HeadyEx's multi-provider infrastructure pool — sourced from GCP, CoreWeave, Lambda Labs, and RunPod, normalized to a common H100-equivalent GPU-hour unit. Buyers use GBUs for large batch inference jobs, model fine-tuning runs, or embedding generation at scale. Sellers are infrastructure providers with excess capacity bidding into the pool.

markets.name: Data Streams
markets.description: Real-time and batch data stream subscriptions from curated third-party data providers — financial market data, web crawl feeds, news aggregators, social media firehoses, IoT sensor streams, and proprietary research datasets. Data Streams are sold as monthly subscriptions with pricing per GB of data delivered, per API call, or per event — depending on the data type and provider model.

markets.name: Agent Labor
markets.description: Agent Labor Units (ALUs) represent the output capacity of specialized Heady AI agents available for hire on the exchange. Organizations with surplus agent capacity — trained, domain-specialized agents not running at full utilization — can list ALUs on the exchange for other organizations to consume. Buyers specify the cognitive archetype, domain specialization, and task type; the exchange matches them to available agents and meters consumption per task or per hour.
markets.unit: Agent Labor Unit (ALU) — agent-hour or per-task
